name: Rioja Alta Wine Trails Loop
name_original: Camino del Vino - circular desde Cihuri por el Castillo de Sajazarra
description: This easy 14.0-mile (22.5 km) touring cycling route from Cihuri explores La Rioja's vineyards and the historic Sajazarra Castle.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cihuri is characterized by routes through extensive vineyards, which are a prominent feature of the Rioja Alta region. The area benefits from a microclimate influenced by the Ebro Valley and sheltered by the Montes Obarenes and Sierra de Cantabria mountains. Two rivers, the Tirón and the Oja, converge nearby, creating natural areas with fluvial vegetation. Best touring cycling routes around Cihuri The most popular touring cycling route is Rioja Alta Wine Trails Loop, an easy 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route allows cyclists to explore the region's vineyards and visit the historic Sajazarra Castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Oja River Greenway – Ezcaray Greenway loop from Casalarreina, a moderate 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This route offers a journey through the regional landscape, mostly on paved surfaces, following an old railway line. Local touring cyclists also love the Puente de Cihuri – The Priory of Cihuri loop from Cihuri, a 22.6 miles (36.3 km) trail leading through local landmarks like the Roman Bridge and Priory House, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Cihuri is defined by vineyards, river valleys along the Oja and Tirón, and the surrounding Ebro Valley landscape. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy vineyard loops to moderate routes exploring regional greenways. The routes in Cihuri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1,300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cihuri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cihuri?
A: There are over 120 touring cycling routes around Cihuri, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the region's vineyards, river valleys, and historical landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cihuri?
A: Cihuri benefits from a unique microclimate, sheltered by the Montes Obarenes and Sierra de Cantabria mountains and influenced by the Ebro Valley. This makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cihuri?
A: Yes, Cihuri offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994473034/rioja-alta-wine-trails-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alta Wine Trails Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.0-mile (22.5 km) trail that takes you through picturesque vineyards and to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the options for more experienced touring cyclists?
A: For more experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 36 difficult routes available around Cihuri. These routes often feature more significant elevation changes and cover longer distances, providing a rewarding experience through the diverse regional landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cihuri?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Cihuri are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31887456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Cihuri – The Priory of Cihuri loop from Cihuri&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.6 miles (36.3 km) and explores local landmarks like the Roman Bridge and Priory House.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cihuri?
A: The region around Cihuri offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find stunning vistas from locations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2985857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4035773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalrayo&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic views of the vineyards, river valleys, and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or attractions to see along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Cihuri is rich in history. You can cycle past the ancient Roman Bridge, an Asset of Cultural Interest, and the 17th-century Priory House. Many routes also lead to impressive castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Velasco&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Cihuri?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cihuri are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque vineyards, the tranquil riverbanks of the Tirón and Oja, and the well-maintained paths, especially those following old railway lines like the Oja-Tirón Ruta Verde.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: Yes, Cihuri is part of the Oja-Tirón Ruta Verde, an itinerary that follows an old railway line. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oja River Greenway – Ezcaray Greenway loop from Casalarreina&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.3-mile (55.2 km) path that offers a pleasant journey through the regional landscape, mostly on paved surfaces, utilizing these greenways.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the local wine region and vineyards?
A: Yes, touring cycling in Cihuri is characterized by routes through extensive vineyards, which are a prominent feature of the Rioja Alta region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994473034/rioja-alta-wine-trails-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alta Wine Trails Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, specifically designed to take you through these acclaimed wine-producing areas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the 'Barrio de las Bodegas'?
A: While specific routes directly through the 'Barrio de las Bodegas' (winery district) are not detailed, many routes traverse the surrounding vineyards. The 'Barrio de las Bodegas' itself is a unique attraction with traditional underground cellars, offering a glimpse into the region's winemaking heritage and opportunities to enjoy local cuisine and wine after your ride.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling trails in Cihuri?
A: While popular routes like the Rioja Alta Wine Trails are well-loved, the region offers 129 routes in total. Exploring some of the less-trafficked routes, particularly those that venture into the rural areas south of the Montes Obarenes and Peñas de Jembres, can provide a more serene cycling experience away from the main trails.

Q: What kind of natural features will I encounter on the routes?
A: You will encounter diverse natural features, including extensive vineyards, limestone soil landscapes, and the 'green heart' created by the convergence of the Tirón and Oja rivers. The riverbanks are populated with leafy alders and willows, offering fantastic examples of natural life and tranquil scenery.
tips: Elegant temple of Baroque work from the 18th century that stands next to the Tirón River. The church was built between 1753 and 1766 with stone blocks extracted from the quarries near the municipality. It stands out for its unique façade where curved elements are used that manage to convey the sensation of movement and dynamism. Right in the center of the façade rises a tower with an octagonal section body with columns.

Gothic-style castle, dating from the late fourteenth century, located in the center of the Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón municipality, in La Rioja Alta.
Built with ashlar masonry from the region, the castle stands out for its square-plan keep. In addition, the fortification took advantage of the river as a defense ditch.
Although you cannot visit its interior, it is worth contemplating it from the outside while walking along the banks of the Tirón River.

In the town of Sajazarra there is a magnificent walled castle.

Between the 12th and 13th centuries the town was fortified. In its beginnings there was a single tower that was destroyed to build, during the second half of the fifteenth century, the current castle.

In the center of the fortress is the keep and, although it is not currently visible, it is believed that there was also a defensive moat.

Following the winemaking tradition of the area, today this beautiful complex made up of the castle, the fortification, the Roman bridge and its gardens, is a winery.

Beautiful Roman bridge that crosses the river Tirón, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, built with ashlar stone from the area. The structure has two semicircular arches and one pointed arch.

The bridge was built during the century and redone in the Middle Ages to maintain its great beauty. Over time, different restorations have been carried out to maintain its functionality.

Next to the bridge stands the Priory house, a Baroque building from the mid-17th century.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route, covering approximately 22.5 kilometers (14 miles), typically takes around 1 hour and 37 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. Its easy difficulty and mostly flat terrain make it a pleasant ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy and is very suitable for beginners and cyclists of all skill levels. It features largely flat terrain with gentle inclines and is predominantly paved, making it accessible and enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, the "Camino del Vino - circular from Cihuri via Sajazarra Castle" is a circular route. You will start and end in Cihuri, making it convenient for parking and exploration.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Cihuri?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas in Cihuri are not detailed, as a starting point for a circular route, you can typically find public parking within the village. Look for designated parking zones or areas near the town center that allow for easy access to the route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is predominantly paved, offering a smooth ride for touring bicycles. You will encounter largely flat sections with only gentle inclines, contributing to its easy difficulty rating. While most of the surface is paved, some broader routes in the Rioja area can include unpaved segments, but this specific Cihuri-Sajazarra loop is noted for being mostly paved.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I see?
faqs.answer: You will be immersed in the quintessential landscapes of La Rioja. The route winds through expansive vineyards, offers views of the Tirón and Oja rivers, and features diverse natural beauty, including fluvial vegetation along the riverbanks. You'll also pass through historic village settings and see traditional wineries.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The La Rioja region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and the vineyards are either budding or displaying their vibrant fall colors. Summer can be warm, while winter might be chilly, but the paved and easy nature of the route makes it accessible for much of the year.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Camino del Vino circular route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. The highlight is the impressive 15th-century Sajazarra Castle, standing majestically over the village of Sajazarra. You may also pass by the Church of San Miguel of Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón, Castillo de los Velasco, and the historic Puente de Cihuri.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or walking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with official trails such as the Sendero del vino A-3, Sendero del vino A-9, and the Ruta Verde del Tirón, among others like Sendero del vino A-4 and Camino Regorreta.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is mostly paved and passes through open countryside and villages, specific regulations for dogs on bicycles or in public spaces can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in populated areas and be mindful of local rules, especially when passing through vineyards or private properties. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling and bring water for them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route. You can enjoy the Camino del Vino circular from Cihuri via Sajazarra Castle freely.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or water along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through charming villages like Sajazarra, which is known for its historic center and wineries. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and potentially wineries offering refreshments and local products. Cihuri, as the starting point, also offers amenities.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel of Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón
